title: Samreen Aulakh
text: Samreen Aulakh is a student at California State University, Monterey Bay http://csumb.edu.

title: About
text:
About Samreen
Samreen is an undergraduate student interested in understanding ecosystem functions and services, and strives to implement conservation efforts in effective ways through research.
There are a number of different ways she works towards improved conservation efforts, most of which include learning and better understanding the field of Marine Science, and the overarching biological sciences as a whole. Although Marine Science includes chemical and physical sciences as well, she is interested in the biological effect of the ocean and its rapidly changing conditions on the marine life that relies on it.

title: Samreen Aulakh
text:
Education
California State University, Monterey Bay
B.S. Marine Science

title: Samreen Aulakh
text:
Experience
Coastal Dune Restoration Project:
Fall 2023 - Spring 2024
U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service, CA State Parks and Habitat Stewardship Project, Monterey Bay (HSP) joint effort in implementing ecosystem and habitat restoration efforts in the Monterey Bay region replanting native coastal dune plants, removal of iceplant. Continuation of restoration efforts ongoing since 2016.
Habitat Stewardship Project, Monterey Bay:
Fall 2024
Refined science communication skills educating and leading individuals aged middle school and beyond, while directly restoring habitat in Monterey county.
Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (SPCA) Monterey County:
Fall 2022
Caretaking of sheltered animals, site maintenance of SPCA, applied science communication with visitors and staff.
Course-based Undergraduate Research Experience (CURE) Faculty Fellowship Program:
Fall 2025
Developed enhanced data analysis techniques, increased problem solving skills, furthered science communication, increased confidence in conducting rigorous research projects, created habits in reading about and participating in scientific research, learned to interact in more constructive ways with peers when discussing or conducting research.
Estuary Monitoring of Battery Emissions and Residues (EMBER):
Spring 2026
Applied data collection and field procedures to help researchers and analysts better understand Elkhorn Slough battery fire impacts, worked in team setting, refined data collection techniques.
